titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Peter Schjeldahl Papers
titlestmt.author: by Tina Evans and Elise Riley

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 5 published digital objects.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 18, 2025 at 00:28

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Ltd. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Correspondence, 1917-2022. II. Writings, 1963-2015. III. Photographs, 1920-2019. IV. Printed material, 1966-2007. V. Professional Papers, 1963-2020. VI. Personal Papers, 1900-2023. VII. Audiovisual materials, 1971-1978. VIII. Computer media, undated.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Peter Schjeldahl was an American art critic and poet. He was born in Fargo, North Dakota, in 1942 and lived in North Dakota and Minnesota. He was educated at Carleton College and The New School. He began his writing career as a reporter for The Jersey Journey before going on to work as an art critic for ARTNews, the New York Times, the New York Times Magazine, Vogue, Vanity Fair. He also served as the head art critic for The New Yorker magazine. Schjeldahl published many essays, articles, and books of poetry.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Peter Schjeldahl Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains correspondence, writings, photographs, personal papers, printed material, audiotapes, computer media, and other papers by or relating to the life and work of Peter Schjeldahl.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Peter Schjeldahl Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Schjeldahl family members, Robert Bly, Ted Berrigan, Joe Brainard, Tom Clark, Ray di Palma, Martha Diamond, Thomas Disch, Dick Gallup, John Giorno, Bernadette Meyer, Ron Padgett, Johen Koethe, Gerald Malanga, and other writers and colleagues.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Original order of correspondence has been retained and the contents of the files are unsorted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains fan letters to Peter Schjeldahl.

did.unittitle: Rich, C. H. to Lee Crabtree

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters to Lee Crabtree and a notebook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings by Peter Schjeldahl as well as a subseries of writings by others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

did.unittitle: Some notes on the art of collage
Words are grown tenuous now, like easels
Ashbery, John: Rivers and mountains, uncorrected proof
Benson, Steve: Quaker Oats box effect
Berrigan Ted and Padgett, Ron: Answer yes or no and then expound a little
Brainard, Joe: Where I am now
Collins, Doug W.: Lichtenstein in suite being poems for Roy Lichtenstein
Dennis, Donna: Little Tube house and the night sky
Myles, Eve: On Peter Schjeldahl since 1964

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an oversized printed version of Body pressure.

did.unittitle: Rauschenberg, John: Poems by John Giorno
S., J.: Peter Schjeldahl in New York
Schneeman, George: Four poems entitled seasons
Vic is admitted to the hospital

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this folder are unsorted as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings by Dick Gallup, Frank Lima, John Perreault, Ted Berrigan, Ron Padgett, and other works by unidentified authors and related photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this folder are unsorted as receieved.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains works by David Schapiro, John Perreault, Jim Brodey, Tom Clark, Lee Harwood, Harris Schiff, and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into four subseries: I. Photographs, 1920-2009. II. Contact sheets, 2019, undated. III. Negatives, 2004, undated. IV. Slides, 1994.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains photographs of Peter Schjeldahl, the Schjeldahl family and others along with photographs taken by Peter Schjeldahl.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains oversize contact sheet of fireworks .jpegs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs taken by Christine Schjeldahl.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Peter Schjeldahl, and members of the Schjeldahl family, including one photograph taken by H. Kanowitz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs taken by Peter Schjeldahl.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photocopies of photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Peter Schjeldahl, the Schjeldahl family, friends, and colleagues.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains oversize photographs of Peter Schjeldahl, fireworks, and one photograph of unidentified people by Harry Rosen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains unidentified photographs, contact sheets, and negatives.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Photographs, contact sheets, and negatives grouped together as originally received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Peter Schjeldahl and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains unidentified contact sheets.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Contact sheets and negatives grouped together as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains unidentified contact sheets and negatives grouped together as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains unidentified negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains slides and negatives of North Dakota and unidentified people.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged alphabetically by type.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains announcements, magazines, posters, and clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains oversize announcements for exhibitions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains unsorted announcements as received of poetry readings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains oversize announcements for poetry readings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains printed material relating to Peter Schjeldahl.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a poster for Levine's Restaurant by Peter Schjeldahl.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains files relating to professional work and legal papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains artwork, juvenalia, vital records, and other personal papers.

did.unittitle: Jeanne: untitled ink and wash drawing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an unidentified oversize painting.

did.unittitle: Unidentified artist: untitled painting inscribed to Brooke and Peter.
Visitor's guide to Washington D. C.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The tapes are arranged chronologically by creation date.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains audio tapes for a proposed biography of the poet, Frank O'Hara. The tapes contain interviews and conversations with friends and collegues conducted in the mid 1970s. The sticky notes affixed to the tapes are in the hand of Ada Calhoun written during the process of writing Also a poet: Frank O'Hara, My father, and me.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: 1 Dr Grams-Greene St. Loft 1972 Interview Three Kathleen ? 2

accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: City Junket excerpts -John Ashbery reading Museum Speech, Translator Act One-scene 4 -Kenward Emslie reading in "The Garden Factory" Act One, Scene Seven May -74 City Junket Excerpts John Ashbery "Translator speech Kenward Emslie "The Garden Factory" Stereo City Junket Excerpts Museum Speech & Garden Party
